evals.prompt: Scan this repo for hardcoded secrets and give me a markdown security report
evals.expected_output: Markdown report titled '# Security Audit Report' with severity-grouped sections (Critical/High/Medium/Low), finding IDs like C-001 or H-001, redacted matches (never full secret values), and remediation guidance for each finding

assertions.text: Report begins with '# Security Audit Report' heading
Report contains at least one finding ID in format [C|H|M|L]-NNN (e.g. C-001, H-002)
No raw secret values are present — matched values are redacted (contain '...' or '****')
Each finding includes a Remediation field with actionable advice
Report ends with a Scan Metadata section

evals.prompt: Run an incremental security audit on files changed since main and give me the results
evals.expected_output: The skill detects git availability, runs in incremental mode using git diff vs main, and produces a report covering only changed files. If no changed files are found, it says so clearly rather than scanning everything.

assertions.text: Output references incremental mode or git diff (phrases like 'incremental', 'changed files', or 'base: main')
Output does not silently fall back to a full scan without mentioning it
Output is either a structured report or an explicit 'no findings' / 'no changed files' message
assertions.value: (?i)(# Security Audit Report|No security findings|No files to scan|No changed files)

evals.prompt: Audit this codebase for security issues and output the results as SARIF so I can upload to GitHub Code Scanning
evals.expected_output: Valid SARIF 2.1.0 JSON with 'version', 'runs', 'tool.driver', 'rules', and 'results' keys. Rules have 'security-severity' properties. Results have 'partialFingerprints' for deduplication. No markdown output — pure JSON.

assertions.text: Rules include 'security-severity' property for GitHub Code Scanning severity mapping
Results include 'partialFingerprints' for deduplication across scans
Output is valid JSON (parseable without errors)

evals.prompt: Check if there are any secrets or credentials hardcoded in my project — just give me a quick summary
evals.expected_output: Skill still triggers and runs a full scan, but tailors the output to be a concise summary: counts by severity, top findings, and a note about how to get the full report. Should NOT skip scanning because the user said 'quick'.

assertions.text: Output shows evidence of actual scanning (mentions files scanned, pattern count, or finding count)
Output does not contain any unredacted secret-like values (long alphanumeric strings > 20 chars not in a code block context)
assertions.guidance: Reviewer should check that no raw API keys, tokens, or passwords appear in plain text
